Header Module(2)


Risk Management Dashboard

Tech Stack Used

FrontendReact with MUI theme
Backend DatabaseMongoDB with Node.js
Hosting serviceNetlify and Render
SDK usedCursor AI & codex
User Interface Google Material Design with Light & Dark theme

App Structure

multiple charts maybe saved under each project
Project
 └── Charts
      ├── Chart 1
      ├── Chart 2
      └── Chart N

Feature Enhancements

under each user → multiples proects, under each proects → multiple dashboard, under each dashboard → multiples charts
User
 └── Organizations
      └── Projects
           └── Dashboards
                └── Charts
1. Add New Chart Module

1. Screenshot

2. Tech Stack used

  • React (state + step flow)
  • React MUI (accordion/inputs/)
  • Native Elements (Input Field/dropdown)

Requirement & Tech Approach Mapping

3. Requirement4. Tech Approach
Capture requirement step by step 
Step 1
Source Link 
1. Capture source link
2. View data from source link
3. Add source link to a list
4. Delete source & View source data		Create a chart using the stepper flow: (Add Data → Chart type → Mapping → Chart Name)
1. Validate the source link, then add to the project list of sources in the database
2. List all the source links
3. View source link data in a pop-up
4. Delete the source link with a confirmation dialogue
Step 2
Select Chart Type
1. Choose the type of chart		Select Chart Type
1. Hardcoded data selection will be saved in the local state management
Step 3
Map Data
1. Select the type of labels and values
2. Select Label and corresponding values to be shown in the chart		Map Data
1. Select the type of labels and values
2. Select Label and corresponding values
Step 4
Chart Name
1. Enter chart name
2. Publish final chart config to dashboard		New Chart Name
1. Get the name from the user input and save in the
local state management
Publish Chart - Create and add the chart to the dashboardAdd all configurations to a new chart in the dashboard to the relevant project via API call

5. Challenges faced

  • Handling multi-step validation
  • Managing the state when going forward between steps
  • Reset the steps when going back between steps

6. Future Enhancements

  • Add draft save
  • Improve error messages
  • MUI style apply for the elements
  • Each step save to DB before going to next step

7. What I learned from this

  • Requirement gathering and flow for frontend and backend also must
2. Add Data Source Module

1. Screenshot

2. Tech Stack used

  • React (state + fetch)
  • MUI (input, modal, list)

Requirement & Tech Approach Mapping

3.REQUIREMENT4.TECH APPROACH
Capture API link and preview json data before savingUsed input state to capture API URL, On add, fetch data and show preview in popup, Validate basic URL and check JSON response, Once confirmed Save to store in DB
Multiple sources can be added for reference, it will be stored in DB so, another new chart within the same project can use it, Ability to add another data with another API end point.Each validated source will be stored in DB, so within the same project another new chart using a different source can be created.
Select source to continue with next accordion stepUsed input state to capture API URL

5. Challenges faced

  • Duplicate source issue per project
  • Data lost when stored in the state
  • Large JSON preview is not fast

6. Future Enhancements

  • Add API auth (headers / token)
  • Better preview UI instead of raw JSON

7. What I learned from this

  • At initial setup must like UI framework, fonts, theme
3. Mapping Module (Reusable Component)

1. Screenshot

2. Tech Stack Used

Tools & technologies implemented in this feature

  • React
    → shared component + state
  • MUI
    → Modal
  • Native table and dropdown

Requirement & Tech Approach Mapping

3.REQUIREMENT4.TECH APPROACH
Show Select Label and value field from API dataFetch API data and list fields
Show selected data preview and allow selectionFetch API data and list fields
Use cases:
1. As stepper while creating new chart
2. As Pop-up while editing existing chart
1. Built a reusable mapping component
2. Used in both stepper flow and edit popup

5. Challenges Faced

Key difficulty during implementation

  • Making same reusable component work in two different places
    → like a component inside an accordion
    → and popup inside dashboard

6. Future Enhancements

Planned improvements

  • Add search for fields

7. What I learned from this

  • Reusable components sharing data by different form
  • data handing
4. Edit Chart Name Module

1. Screenshot

2. Tech Stack used

  • Material UI Popup

Requirement & Tech Approach Mapping

3. REQUIREMENT4. TECH APPROACH
Click 3-dot menu on a chart to open
Open popup to edit chart name		Open a pop-up from the chart card
Show existing chart name in the input
Allow user to update name and save		Use a state to handle input.
Close popup after clicked “save” button
Refresh chart list for updated changes		API handling update name to chat via ID

5. Challenges faced

  • After saving the chart name, bring the edited name to the corresponding chart

6. Future Enhancements

  • Validation – new names allow only text and numbers, don’t allow other characters or symbols or empty fields.

7. What I learned from this

  • identification through name, for clarity approach to avoid future confusions
5. Dashboard Module

1. Screenshot

2. Tech Stack used

  • Used Chart.js npm package - configured for the chart

Requirement & Tech Approach Mapping

3. REQUIREMENT4. TECH APPROACH
Navigation Back button, page titleNavigation to previous to project module
Navigation add new chart buttonNavigation to the previous to add new chart module
chart name, kebab icon with menu optionsApi integration
Edit chart Name nav to the relevant popup, Edit the chart Data nav to the relevant popupchart build

5. Challenges faced

  • Api integration and Modifying data to chart configuration

6. Future Enhancements

  • Responsive model
  • Switch option switch to other project
  • Api handling into separate file structure

7. What I learned from this

  • charts show better visualization of data each ups and downs
6. Header Module

1. Screenshot

2. Tech Stack used

  • Semantic-release npm package used and configured

Requirement & Tech Approach Mapping

3. REQUIREMENT4. TECH APPROACH
Show logo and app versionVersion numbers are automatically incremented from the code repository using Git commit messages and the semantic-release npm package, following semantic versioning rules for major, minor, and patch updates.
Navigation between pagesused react router
Toggle themes (Dark/Light)toggle using global state and saved in localStorage
Show Date - Search - Notification - Status, Settings Icon(feature)Used MUI components for stylings
Keep same header in all pagescreated one common header component and used in all pages

5. Challenges faced

  • Build Responsive UI,
  • Tracking versions only through commit messages can be confusing. Instead, using the same semantic version for both Frontend and Backend, tied to a single Git tag through semantic-release, gives a clearer and more consistent understanding of each release.

6. Future Enhancements

  • Text into logo image
  • Setting icon adds navigation link after setting page completed.

7. What I learned from this

  • versioning is important to track down feature and related bugs. without versioning total commit list is mess up
7. Projects List Module

1. Screenshot

2. Tech Stack used

  • MongoDB + Node

Requirement & Tech Approach Mapping

3. REQUIREMENT4. TECH APPROACH
Show list of projectsUsed React to render project list from backend data
Create new project, Rename existing onesConnected to backend APIs (/projects) for create, update, delete
Delete project with related chartsStored project data in mongoDB and fetched using API
Click project name to navigate to dashboardNavigation handled using React router to move to dashboard page
Create new project (validation), Rename existing ones (validation)Used unique name validation before saving new project

5. Challenges faced

  • Building Responsive UI,
  • Validation – for uniqueness in name, it prevents duplicate names
  • Api integration

6. Future Enhancements

  • Validation – new names allow only text and number, don’t allow other characters or symbols or empty fields.
  • Enhancement – edit in direct item itself.
  • Api handling into separate file structure

7. What I learned from this

  • Every management has a head, likewise charts is connected and managed relevant to the project head.
